steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The equation $\frac{x+5}{3} = \frac{x+5}{12}$ has the same numerator on both sides but different denominators. The only way two fractions with equal numerators and unequal denominators can be equal is if the numerator is $0$. So $x + 5 = 0$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'between 2 and 6': does not contain 0. ✗
  • Choice B — 'between −2 and 2': contains 0. ✓
  • Choice C — 'between −6 and −3': does not contain 0. ✗
  • Choice D — 'between 7 and 12': does not contain 0. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
